qual1: Bad spelling, appearing out of nowhere without prior reason to, psychological pressures within the message.
qual2: Often due to convenience or possibly due to lack of knowledge.
qual3: Email address and information within the message is consistent with others from a trusted organisation, when it seems plausible to receive a message, when a message is sent in response to an actual event.
qual4: If I have seen that the information seems legitimate and appears to have come from a reputable person or organisation and that the details all 'line up'

qual1: Things asking you to follow a link, asking for bank details, a slight variation in the email
qual2: Pressure, stress, over confidence
qual3: Expected email, not asking to follow a link, asking you to contact them so you can go through security questions etc
qual4: The body of the email and what it is asking

qual1: Unexpected messages / e-mails, those with links are even more so suspicious. E-mails claiming to be from one party but the e-mail address being something different.
qual2: Attackers / bad actors only need to get a very small percentage of their target pool to click / take action. They can cast very wide nets and see who gets tricked, their targets need to be right 100% of the time to not become a victim.
qual3: For most services, if it isn't already flagged as spam I'm more likely to take the time to read it, and from there it depends on if I know the person / company and if it reads as a legitimate message or something likely spammy.
qual4: By default I ignore, I'll only reply if the message seems legitimate and actually needs a reply. If in doubt and I know who the person is I'll contact them through another channel to confirm that they tried to reach me.

qual1: too good to be true emails. Dodgy emails.
qual2: Lack of understanding
qual3: Email address and content. Not asking for payment/money
qual4: Type of message and who it is from. If I am unsure I will google to check on scams etc.

qual1: Vague, strange wording, weird email address. Just different from emails you get regularly from people you know.
qual2: Mental fatigue, laps in concentration, lack of training or knowing what to look out for
qual3: Knowing the email address. Them not asking me for something â€˜out of the normâ€™. Usually visually the format tends to look â€˜offâ€™.
qual4: If in doubt. Iâ€™ll ignore it or contact the person directly if I know them to check its validity.

qual1: Requests to preform a action with a link. Names acconts I haven't used or didn't set up. Coming from unknown number.
qual2: Time pressure, want to get on with their day, taking the most convenient option
qual3: Detailed instructions, no link, known/registered number
qual4: Trust the sender, needs my input, expect the communication

qual1: Poor grammar, unexpected messages, random email addresses, no name in email, link in message
qual2: A lack of awareness of types of threats to them, or a belief that they would not be targeted
qual3: My name in the message, information that the company should know, MFA for logins, no links in messages but rather directing you to where to go instead
qual4: If the message asks for an action, double checking the validity of the action (is it actually something that I was expecting to be asked to do). If the message does not contain my name then a response is very unlikely.

qual1: When the email address is made up of random letters, number etc and the message or email doesnâ€™t have my name on it.
qual2: Do not realise how good scammers are?!
qual3: If I have bought something from the company in the past
qual4: I ignore most requests/ emails/ texts etc

qual1: If a message is unexpected, unrelated to me, too good to be true, not tailored to me(could be sent in batch) or from an unfamiliar domain
qual2: Convenience. It's faster to not have MFA or different passwords for different accounts. Security takes up time that people usually want to be doing over things. Taking the time to scrutinise an email might feel wasted
qual3: If I'm expecting it, if it comes from an email address or domain that I have received legitimate emails from in the past, if it only directs to me to go a website instead of providing a link
qual4: I assess whether it seems legitimate, whether I have good reason to respond to the message

qual1: Typos
qual2: Not enough awareness

qual4: By analysing the contents of the message

qual1: Unknown number or broken text or lack of personalisation or correct grammar

qual4: I often ignore regardless

qual1: Anything that is too good to be true or out of the blue/overly fiendly

qual3: It would have to be verifiable in real life
qual4: Unless its from someone I know irl I dont answer it

qual1: The email address
qual2: I think people are so confident in themselves and what they think they know about things online it can led to them not paying attention and taking risks
qual3: The name of the company or service makes me feel I trust the source.
qual4: If I feel something is off I wonâ€™t respond

qual1: Spelling mistakes, urgency in the request, AI images, receiving a message from a new number / email claiming to be someone I know
qual2: Multitasking, time pressure, lack of training, scammers evolving their tactics
qual3: The message coming from an official email address, in a format I expect / the same format as previous messages
qual4: By taking into consideration all of the above

qual1: Strange email addresses, time pressure words, stressed urgency, breaches of protocol.
qual2: Time and social pressures, lack of awareness.
qual3: If it comes from someone I know or am expecting, from an email address or point of contact that I'm aware of.
qual4: I normally do it based off my own needs. If I don't know or am not expecting a message from someone, I generally ignore it.

qual1: Spelling errors, suspicious links, unexpected messages, messages/ request that seem unreasonably pressureful/ time urgent.
qual2: Lack of education/awareness in certain areas.
qual3: If I knew/ was expecting it
qual4: If I can trust the sendor, recognise the context of the message, whether the link looks suspicious.

qual1: Unknown sender. When the subject of the email is unfamiliar. When there are spelling mistakes or poor presentation. When they request unusually detailed information. Unnecessary urgency.
qual2: Complacency.
qual3: Reassurance of the authenticity of the sender.
qual4: Whether I recognise the sender or the subject

qual1: Poor grammar, poor English, strange look links trying to pass as something more official, generic introduction in email, narrative trying to encourage a sense of urgency
qual2: Being made to feel that the situation is urgent, caught when busy, lack of training/familiarisation with social engineering methods.
qual3: Known users, having had verbal comms before request, a verification procedure to confirm request within a trusted app.
qual4: If I am expecting the request, if I know the user/source (i.e. not a random mobile number)

qual1: Excessive urgency, poor grammar/formatting.
qual2: Lack of awareness of potential consequences.
qual3: If it were an email, having the correct domain name in the latter half of the @, sticking to previous formatting and meeting expectations, i.e not an urgent "claim NOW" style of surprise.
qual4: If I need to or not really, most emails I get on my personal one are noreply and anything else I ensure is legitimate or not. Work-wise its easier due to filtering and Knowbe4 training.

qual1: Not From a email i Recognise, Bad Grammar, Suspicious links that dont seem to lead where the email says it leads, Spelling mistake in email name, lack of branding / signatures if it says its from a company, if its adressed to me from a company im supposed to have an account with im suspicious if it doesnt say my name or gets my name wrong as the companies i pay subscriptions for etc should have my name already.
qual2: lack of knowledge on online safety or desperation if something seems to good to be true and complacency, sometimes emails come from accounts that have the same emails as a big brand but have one letter changed disguising themselves as that company, if People dont pay attention to those names they may click links etc thinking its from the actual company especially if its from a familiar brand like amazon etc that typically send emails everyday when waiting for a product to arrive.
qual3: if its from a familiar brand, if its related to stuff ive searched online, if its emails that embody what i typically get from brands.
qual4: I dont typically respond or click on things from automated / bot emails, or if they arent personally customised to me,I only typically respond to emails directly adressed / personalised for me.

qual1: incorrect spelling or grammer, weird email origin, unrelated topics
qual2: lack of education or emotional payoff
qual3: coming from a normal formatted email, or from a sender i know, relavant or expected content, no urgency or prompting payement
qual4: do i need to respond? do i have a timeframe in whcih i need to respond?

qual1: broken english, suspicious links, urgency within the email, unfamiliar tone
qual2: because they either arent educated in the threats online or they arent being vigilant enough
qual3: if i had been contacted by them previously
qual4: if it looks suspicious i dont repsond

qual1: Urgency, a request of the reciptent -especially one that is unsual for the sender. Untrustworthy links.
qual2: People get tired of being secure, and they get a false sense of security from the fact that risks often go unpunished.
qual3: Expecting an email makes me most likely to trust an email and perform actions that could be considered unsafe.
qual4: Depends on the requirements for me, if the action is must be done but i don't trust it i will usually ask for guidance from others.

qual1: Typo's, Irregular formatting, unrecognised contacts/emails, wrong information
qual2: Naivety and sometimes overconfidence. The "it would never happen to me" attitude of clicking and downloading things.
qual3: If I had history of genuine emails/texts from that contact and the formatting of the email or text is regular and expected. There is obviously still risk in this due to spoofing capability
qual4: I typically ignore anything I am not expecting from anyone I don't know.

qual1: If it refers to something i don't engage with e.g. message from a bank I don't use
qual2: It takes a lot of time and effort to follow best practice all the time. I think people get fed up with expending all that effort and become more sloppy with their security.
qual3: Spelling grammar is correct, it's from someone I know and it concerns a service I use.
qual4: I check if it even warrants a response i.e is it asking me to do something or is it just informational? I check spelling, hover over links, check it's addressed directly to me,check the sender's name seems legitimate, and think whether the sender would genuinely ask me to complete the action being asked of me.

qual1: I find it difficult to know what it genuine and what isn't
qual2: Sometimes the emails/things I see can look threatening.
qual3: If it's from a service I use.
